title: Humboltia decurrens
intro: Humboldtia decurrens, also known as the 'Malabar tellicherry,' is a plant species native to the Western Ghats region of India, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia decurrens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used to make extracts and capsules for its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Humboltia decurrens is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a type of evergreen climber native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and southern India. The classification of Humboltia decurrens places it within the genus Humboltia, which is a group of tropical plants known for their unique flowers and habits. The plant is also sometimes referred to by its synonym, Cryptostegia decurrens.
